channel.title: Next...Next...Next...Finished
channel.description: A technical blog by some one in IT Security

item.title: Building an MCP server for Tripwire IP360 vulnerability information
item.description: For those of you have been experimenting with AI's recently it's likely you've come across Model Context Protocol (MCP) (for those who haven't, it is effectively a clean way to expose operational systems to an LLM as a structured toolset). There's lot of use cases for this, but…

item.title: Identity is the New OS: Rethinking Security Boundaries in the Cloud
item.description: In traditional infrastructure, the operating system was the security boundary.

item.title: Monitoring Azure Blob Storage with CLI-Based File Integrity Monitoring
item.description: Continuing my previous post about cloud storage monitoring, I'm back with the "Azure edition" today. I'll skip the preamble about why this is important and get straight to the commands this time (but stay tuned about some additional considerations that are relevant to both AWS a…

item.title: Monitoring Amazon S3 with CLI-Based File Integrity Monitoring
item.description: Cloud object storage introduces a subtle but critical challenge for File Integrity Monitoring (FIM): there is no “file system” in the traditional sense. But that doesn't mean you shouldn't be monitoring it (given it's even more exposed than your on-premise architecture in many c…

item.title: Monitoring OpenGaussDB with Tripwire Enterprise
item.description: I've not done much with GaussDB/OpenGaussDB, so figured I'd post the process I used to setup some monitoring including the deployment itself.

item.title: Integrating VirusTotal with Fortra's Tripwire Enterprise in two minutes
item.description: One of Tripwire Enterprise's greatest strengths is the ability to quickly and easily take action in response to changes either automatically or during a review by an administrator... and one of the most common requests I see from clients is "how can I know whether this is a good…

item.title: Monitoring Containers - A FIM perspective
item.description: One of the common asks I get as a consultant at the moment is how best to approach monitoring containers with "FIM"... but modern Kubernetes/container platforms (e.g. Red Hat OpenShift ) change a lot of the assumptions that traditional host-based security monitoring relied on si…

item.title: Architecture Anti-Patterns
item.description: File Integrity Monitoring (FIM) and Security Configuration Management (SCM) platforms like the ones I currently work with at Fortra are deceptively simple on the surface: deploy/configure endpoints, define rules/assessments, schedule tasks, evaluate results, report.

item.title: TE External Rule Elements Example
item.description: One of the most powerful things I do with Tripwire Enterprise is add external sources to TE's change audit tracking. Whether it's an API, or a device with an unsupported SSH config, this workflow enables me to capture data and bring it along side all the rest of the compliance a…

item.title: Trigger a scan with the CCM REST API
item.description: On the theme of recent posts about scripting (and automating scanning - if you're still doing this manually, you're doing something wrong IMHO!), here's a useful script I put together to help demo how to trigger a compliance scan with Tripwire's Configuration Compliance Manager

item.title: Google's Unsigned APK Crackdown
item.description: I'm late to publicly comment on this hot topic (and do my best not to post "opinions" online in general!), but I thought I'd offer a personal story on Google's moves to required app developers provide personal ID to back the signing of all apps that run on Android (for those who…

item.title: Posting a Tripwire IP360 ASPL update with Python
item.description: This one initially threw me, so I figured I'd post the script here for others in case they run into issues:

item.title: Change Audit for Network Devices
item.description: I often post over on the Tripwire/Fortra bIog about the importance of monitoring, but don't typically talk about the "technical ins and outs" of how I approach and think about Change Audit so I'm hoping to start a series of posts about File Integrity Monitoring (FIM) aka Change…

item.title: Configuring IP360 Windows targets via group policy
item.description: Group Policy is the typical/preferred method of setting many of the requirements for Tripwire IP360 vulnerability scanning and whilst the official endpoint guide is helpful with identifying various settings, it can take time to map many of the settings to the specific GPO settin…

item.title: IP360 API with Python - Work Experience Case Study
item.description: The following was created to test a work experience student's Python skills! It was prior to the official implementation of the REST API, so uses the XML interface and as a result some of this is now slightly dated (using API Explorer for example) but it can still potentially be…

item.title: Initiating a Vulnerability Scan with IP360 Rest API and PowerShell
item.description: One of the most common things I do with Tripwire's IP360 in Professional Services it automate workflows. Nearly everyone wants a way to quickly trigger a scan (be it on detection of a new machine or a request for further data in a security playbook) and it's nice and easy to do…

item.title: Microsoft PowerBi and Tripwire Enterprise
item.description: A common request I get is setting up Microsoft PowerBi with Tripwire Enterprise. Whilst we don't have an official "out-of-the-box" solution for this, TE's REST API makes this a pretty simple option to get started with even if you just want to use PowerBi's native web reporting:

item.title: Setting up your lab Tripwire Enterprise with Tailscale
item.description: One of the most common things I need in my lab is access to the web console for Tripwire Enterprise to do demos- but it can be a nuisance to demonstrate with a VM quickly if I'm out on the road... until Tailscale quietly made homelabs easy to remotely setup. Now, I don't even ne…

item.title: FIM, Auditing, and Configuration Compliance in Active-Active and Active-Passive Designs
item.description: In a lot of environments I walk into, clustering is treated as a resilience problem - but not always as a security one. We're busy designing for uptime, failover, scale, etc... but we don’t always design for consistency of trust across the cluster (especially for on premise depl…

item.title: Building a Vulnerability Management Program from Scratch
item.description: A post on Vulnerability Management this week on the Tripwire blog: https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/building-vulnerability-management-program-scratch

item.title: What's Next for Cybersecurity in 2025 and Beyond? Fortra Experts Weigh In
item.description: I got a comment in the Fortra blog today about 2025 -

item.title: The Top 10 State of Security Blog Posts From 2024
item.description: I made it to a top ten list: https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/top-state-security-blog-posts for my post: Bake Security Into Your Cyber Kitchen. I love writing a top ten list, but being a part of one is even more exciting- you can read the original post here: https://ww…

item.title: How to Integrate FIM with SOAR Platforms
item.description: I have another new blog up on the Tripwire State of Security site: https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/how-integrate-fim-soar-platforms

item.title: Security Automation – As Easy As Making Tea?
item.description: I can't deny, I always like a metaphor that makes sense to me - hence my latest blog over on the Tripwire site: https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/security-automation-easy-making-tea

item.title: Guest Comment on the Tripwire blog
item.description: I got to contribute to the Tripwire blog again this week with a quick comment on Cybersecurity Frameworks: What Do the Experts Have to Say? https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/cybersecurity-frameworks-what-do-experts-have-say

item.title: Making Windows Terminal Look nice in 2024
item.description: I recently had to rebuild a PC recently and whilst a lot of my settings automatically came across (thanks OneDrive backup) my poor Windows Terminal did not do well with a profile migration. Setting up Windows in 2024 to work "nicely" with OH-MY-POSH (especially if your profile i…

item.title: VMWare Workstation Pro - for free (for personal use!)
item.description: Whilst I'd love to say every app I want to run in my home lab works well with Docker or similar, it's often the case I end up using a full VM for emulation - and VMware workstation has remained a solid option for this for years (especially around running a number of high resourc…

item.title: Bake-off: Ensuring Security in the Cyber Kitchen
item.description: The Bear (on Disney+ in the UK) has gotten to me (and my blog post): https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/bake-ensuring-security-cyber-kitchen

item.title: Where Security Starts in Your Security Projects
item.description: Security starts... by reading my latest blog post over on the Tripwire blog https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/where-security-starts-your-security-projects !

item.description: Another Tripwire blog post for me this week: https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/know-thyself-and-thy-network

item.title: Change Variance: How Tiny Differences Can Impact Your IT World
item.description: File Integrity Monitoring and Change Audit is the two words I use the most in a week - and this blog explores Change Variance over at Tripwire: https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/change-variance-how-tiny-differences-can-impact-your-it-world

item.title: A Scary Story of Group Policy Gone Wrong: Accidental Misconfigurations
item.description: Does everyone have one of these: https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/scary-story-group-policy-gone-wrong-accidental-misconfigurations ?

item.title: Is secure-by-default an achievable reality?
item.description: I want to believe it's possible - but is it? https://www.tripwire.com/state-of-security/secure-default-achievable-reality

item.title: Place your budgets on the right cybersecurity for your business
item.description: One area where security can deliver cost-savings to the business is by speeding up incident response . Though, achieving more efficient incident response may require some strategizing. Recurring incidents like AV detections or firewall intrusion alerts can be expedited by ensuri…

item.title: Integrating IP360 with AWS Lambda
item.description: I built the below script to allow users to easily scale our vulnerability management monitoring of servers deployed in AWS with automated exceptions being pulled from an S3 bucket and automatically added to Tripwire IP360 via a AWS Lambda call:
